description: Generate documentation for a repo from scratch using the Diátaxis framework (organise around USER NEEDS via the four-type compass — tutorials / how-to / reference / explanation — not around the product's file structure). Spawns: root epic -> a single discover spawner -> one doc bead per manifest item (created at runtime, classified by the compass) -> a finalize bead gated on all the discovered docs. Re-bases the legacy flowdoc-generate 'features-not-files' taxonomy onto Diátaxis (ADR flowdoc-yuw / decisions/0001). Pour with --var audience=maintainer (default) or --var audience=user. Run via 'bd mol pour diataxis-generate --var audience=...'.

audience.description: maintainer = developer/contributor docs in diataxis/maintainer/ (implementation detail, source paths, code, mermaid, API/View tables allowed); user = end-user docs in diataxis/user/ (NO code, NO source paths, NO internal URLs; task/outcome-oriented voice). The audience overlay constrains the SURFACE DETAIL of each doc; it never overrides a Diátaxis type's orientation.

steps.title: Diátaxis: generate {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a from-scratch Diátaxis documentation pass ({{audience}} edition).

Diátaxis organises docs around FOUR USER NEEDS, located on a 2×2 compass
(research/tech/diataxis-documentation-framework-research.md; ADR flowdoc-yuw):
  Navigate by two questions — action or cognition? acquisition or application?
    - action  + acquisition  -> tutorial      (learning-oriented)
    - action  + application  -> how-to guide   (goal/task-oriented)
    - cognition + application -> reference      (information-oriented)
    - cognition + acquisition -> explanation    (understanding-oriented)
This REPLACES the legacy features/flows/endpoints/views/concepts taxonomy,
which was product-led — the exact failure mode Diátaxis cures.

AUDIENCE MAP (read this first, every child obeys it):
  - audience = maintainer -> write to diataxis/maintainer/ ; voice = for
    developers/contributors; may include implementation detail, source file
    paths, code blocks, mermaid, API/View tables, internal/build commands.
  - audience = user       -> write to diataxis/user/       ; voice = for end
    users; NO source paths, NO code, NO internal/localhost/staging URLs, NO dev-setup/build/test instructions; task/outcome oriented.
The audience overlay constrains a doc's allowed surface detail only — it never
overrides a type's Diátaxis orientation.

This epic has exactly two authored children: 'discover' (runs first and spawns
one bead per documentation item, each classified by the compass) and 'finalize'
(runs last, gated on every doc). Do not work this epic directly — work
its leaves.

steps.title: Diátaxis {{audience}}: discover & scaffold, then spawn one bead per doc
steps.description:
DISCOVERY + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is the value shown as
"Complete beads issue <id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF below.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = diataxis/maintainer/ if maintainer,
diataxis/user/ if user.

== Part A: survey & scaffold (do all of this, then spawn, then stop) ==
1. list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency manifests, configs, entry
   points. Identify tech stack, what the product DOES, the jobs users and
   contributors actually need docs for. You are mining USER NEEDS, not a file
   inventory.
2. Write DOCS_DIR/index.md: a landing page for this audience that links to the
   four Diátaxis sections (tutorials / how-to / reference / explanation) and
   states, in one line each, what each section is FOR. (user edition:
   plain-language, no file paths.)
3. Write DOCS_DIR/_DiataxisGuide.md — the authoring contract every doc bead
   follows. It MUST contain, verbatim, the compass classification rule and the
   four per-type templates below, so a generic agent produces output that
   obeys Diátaxis. Traceable to the research report
   (research/tech/diataxis-documentation-framework-research.md) and ADR
   flowdoc-yuw. Write it EXACTLY like this:

     # _DiataxisGuide.md — authoring contract ({{audience}} edition)

     ## The compass — classify EVERY item before you write it
     Ask two questions, in order:
       Q1. Does the reader need to DO (action) or to KNOW (cognition)?
       Q2. Are they STUDYING (acquisition) or WORKING (application)?
     The pair selects the type:
       action  + acquisition  -> tutorial
       action  + application  -> how-to guide
       cognition + application -> reference
       cognition + acquisition -> explanation
     The compass is a SHOULD/diagnostic, not a MUST: never reject a doc merely
     for 'mixing modalities', but each doc has ONE primary type that decides
     its section folder. (research §'The Compass: Two Axes')

     ## tutorials/ — learning-oriented  (https://diataxis.fr/tutorials/)
       * A lesson under the guidance of a tutor; serves STUDY, learning by doing.
       * RUTHLESSLY MINIMISE EXPLANATION — link out to explanation instead.
       * ASPIRE TO PERFECT RELIABILITY: every step works, every time. The
         author carries nearly all the responsibility.
       * One concrete, achievable, happy-path end-goal. No decision points,
         no 'if you want, you can…'.
       Template: Title / What you'll build / Prerequisites / numbered steps,
       each with the exact result the reader should see / 'You did it' close /
       'Where next' links to how-to + explanation. NO digressions.

     ## how-to/ — goal/task-oriented  (https://diataxis.fr/how-to-guides/)
       * Directions that guide an ALREADY-COMPETENT reader through a REAL task.
       * Recipe-style: a series of steps toward a result, correctly and safely.
       * About GOALS, PROJECTS and PROBLEMS — NOT about tools (tools are
         reference). Assume competence; omit teaching.
       * Troubleshooting/'fix X' content lives here (it is a task).
       Template: 'How to <goal>' / when to use this / numbered recipe steps /
       variations & options / 'Done — verify' / Related (link reference + other
       how-tos). No conceptual lecturing.

     ## reference/ — information-oriented  (https://diataxis.fr/reference/)
       * Technical DESCRIPTION of the machinery and how to operate it (a MAP).
       * DESCRIBE AND ONLY DESCRIBE. No tutorials, no how-to steps, no opinion.
       * PRODUCT-LED, not user-led: structure MIRRORS the product. Style is
         AUSTERE, neutral, objective, factual, consistent.
       Template: subject overview line / structured description — tables for
       commands/variables/parameters/fields/return values, ordered to mirror
       the product / examples ONLY as illustration / Related. No 'why', no
       walkthrough. (maintainer may add source-path / mermaid / API tables;
       user describes only what users operate: CLI, variables, output layout.)

     ## explanation/ — understanding-oriented  (https://diataxis.fr/explanation/)
       * Discursive treatment that permits reflection; answers 'Can you tell me
         about…?'. Serves STUDY — the WHY. The 'read in the bath' kind of doc.
       * MUST provide context and background; MAY and SHOULD consider
         ALTERNATIVES and counter-examples; MAY admit OPINION and perspective.
       * Not tied to a single task; no step-by-step.
       Template: 'About <topic>' / context & background / how it fits together /
       why this approach (tradeoffs, alternatives considered, opinion allowed) /
       Related (link tutorial + reference). Conceptual overviews live here.

     ## Audience overlay ({{audience}}) — surface-detail constraint, layered on top
       * maintainer: implementation detail, source paths, code, mermaid,
         API/View tables, internal/build commands ALL allowed.
       * user: NO source paths, NO code, NO internal/localhost/staging URLs,
         NO dev-setup/build/test instructions. 'Product expert to customer'
         voice. (The maintain formula's leakage grep — src/ | npm  | localhost
         — is the audit gate; do not trip it.)
       The overlay limits allowed detail only; it NEVER overrides a type's
       orientation above.

     ## Anti-pattern (DO NOT) — the documented Diátaxis misuse
       Do NOT pre-create empty tutorials/ how-to/ reference/ explanation/
       folders 'just in case'. From the official guidance: "It certainly does
       not mean that you should create empty structures … with nothing in them.
       Don't do that. It's horrible." A section folder exists ONLY once it
       holds at least one real document. Structure EMERGES per item.
       READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR.

4. Write DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md: progress counters + every item to document,
   grouped BY DIÁTAXIS TYPE (not by features/flows/endpoints/views/concepts).
   Run the compass on each item to assign its type. Use headings
   '## Tutorials', '## How-to', '## Reference', '## Explanation' and list each
   item as:
     "- [ ] NNN | <Type>: <Title> -> [Title](<type>/<slug>.md)"
   where <type> is tutorials | how-to | reference | explanation. OMIT any type
   heading that has zero items (no empty buckets — see the anti-pattern). A
   small repo may legitimately have items in only one or two types.
5. Do NOT create the four type folders yet. They are created lazily by the doc
   beads — a section dir comes into existence only when an item lands in it
   (the anti-pattern rule, encoded as wiring).

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per manifest item (the deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH unchecked item in _Manifest.md, create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=2 --parent=$SELF \
    --title='Diátaxis {{audience}}: <Type>: <Title>' \
    --description='Write DOCS_DIR/<type>/<slug>.md following the <Type>
       template in DOCS_DIR/_DiataxisGuide.md for audience={{audience}}.
       First CONFIRM the type by running the compass (action/cognition?
       acquisition/application?) — if it is wrong, fix the type/path in
       _Manifest.md. Create the <type>/ folder if it does not yet exist
       (lazy creation — no empty buckets). Read the relevant source fresh,
       fill every template section in the type''s orientation (e.g. tutorials
       minimise explanation; reference describes only; explanation may give
       opinion), obey the {{audience}} overlay, link related docs across types,
       then tick this item -> [x] in _Manifest.md and bump the counters.
       Acceptance: file exists in the correct type folder, all template
       sections filled (no placeholders), the doc stays within its single
       Diátaxis orientation, links valid, manifest updated.'
WIRING RULES — violating these creates deadlocks, so obey exactly:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - Do NOT add any dependency from a doc bead to the finalize bead.
  - Do NOT add blocks edges between doc beads — they are independent/parallel.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child (a tiny repo still gets >=1 doc).
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after scaffolding + spawning. Summarize what you wrote, the per-type item
counts, and how many doc beads you spawned so the judges can verify.

steps.title: Diátaxis {{audience}}: finalize docs (cross-links, indexes, compass review)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} Diátaxis documentation set. You only become ready
once 'discover' and EVERY doc bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = diataxis/maintainer/ if maintainer, diataxis/user/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md is checked [x]; fix counters.
2. Verify NO empty type folder exists: every one of tutorials/ how-to/
   reference/ explanation/ that is present holds >=1 real doc; delete any
   folder that ended up empty (the anti-pattern guard).
3. Compass review: skim each doc and confirm it stays within its single
   Diátaxis orientation — tutorials don't bloat into explanation, reference
   stays describe-only, how-to stays task-focused, explanation isn't a
   walkthrough. Note (do NOT silently rewrite) any doc that strays so the
   judges can see it; the compass is a SHOULD, so only flag clear violations.
4. Verify all intra-doc links resolve and DOCS_DIR/index.md plus each present
   section's listing are complete and current.
5. (user edition only) Grep the docs for developer leakage — source paths,
   shell/install commands (src/ | npm  | localhost), staging URLs, class/
   function names — and replace with user-facing equivalents.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.
